title: Cozy Creamy Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring lamb, elderflower, and a crispy en-croute topping, made with ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients. This winey and molten tagine is perfect for a cozy dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the elderflower syrup, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Roll out the puff pastry to a large sheet, about 1/4 inch thick.
Place the pastry over the lamb mixture and press the edges to seal.
Cut a few slits in the top of the pastry to allow steam to escape.
Bake the tagine for 25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the lamb is tender.
